title: Code structure
text: package seven R source files, listed order collated (specified Collate field DESCRIPTION): mizerSeasonal-class.R collated first S4 class definitions available code references . animate.R uses unexported mizer functions (get_time_elements) dplyr verbs imported package level mizerSeasonal-package.R.

what: How the package hooks into mizer
title: Code structure
text: Mizer ships formal extension API. key functions used package : registerExtension(pkgname, ...) — called .onLoad register package mizer extension current R session. setComponent(params, component, initial_value, dynamics_fun) — registers new dynamical state variable (, gonadic mass matrix). setRateFunction(params, rate, fun) — replaces one mizer’s standard rate calculators custom one. Used RDD function; RDI Encounter handled via S3 dispatch instead (see ). coerceToExtensionClass(params) — promotes MizerParams object registered S4 extension class (, mizerSeasonal), enabling S3 method dispatch project* generics. other_params(params) / other_params(params) <- — named list arbitrary configuration attached MizerParams object.

previous_headings: How the package hooks into mizer
title: Code structure
text: runs package loaded tells mizer extension. makes coerceToExtensionClass() know S4 class promote .

previous_headings: How the package hooks into mizer
title: Code structure
text: Two S4 classes defined mizerSeasonal-class.R: marker classes: carry additional slots. sole purpose enable S3 method dispatch project* generics. mizer calls e.g. projectRDI(params, ...), dispatches projectRDI.mizerSeasonal params class mizerSeasonal. Similarly, project() returns mizerSeasonalSim MizerSim objects coerced registered extension sim class.

previous_headings: How the package hooks into mizer
title: Code structure
text: setSeasonalReproduction() wires everything together: Note setRateFunction("RDI", ...) setRateFunction("Encounter", ...) called. Instead, rates intercepted via S3 methods projectRDI.mizerSeasonal projectEncounter.mizerSeasonal described next two sections. RDD function still set via setRateFunction projectRDD.MizerParams already dispatches internally params@rates_funcs$RDD; S3 override needed .
code: setSeasonalReproduction <- function(params,                                     release_func = "seasonalVonMisesRelease",                                     RDD = "seasonalBevertonHoltRDD",                                     include_gonads = TRUE) {     initial <- initialN(params)     initial[] <- 0      other_params(params)$release_func  <- release_func   # <-- config     other_params(params)$include_gonads <- include_gonads      p <- setComponent(params,                            # <-- new state variable                       component = "gonads",                       initial_value = initial,                       dynamics_fun = "gonadDynamics") |>         setRateFunction("RDD", RDD)                      # <-- replace RDD only      p@extensions <- mizer::getRegisteredExtensions()     p <- mizer::coerceToExtensionClass(p)                # <-- promote to mizerSeasonal     return(p) }

previous_headings: How the package hooks into mizer
what: Where the release function name is stored
title: Code structure
text: choice release function stored character string inside other_params(): Internally mizer stores params@other_params$$release_func, always use other_params() accessor rather @ slot directly, slot layout may change across mizer versions. runtime, gonadDynamics() projectRDI.mizerSeasonal() retrieve function : get0() used instead get() returns NULL rather throwing error name found. release_func NULL code fail next line, gives slightly clearer error one get() raise.

previous_headings: How the package hooks into mizer
what: Where the initial gonadic mass lives
title: Code structure
text: initial gonad matrix (zeros) stored : mizer’s standard storage initial state components. set setComponent() project() uses initialise simulation.

title: Code structure
text: gonadDynamics() solves PDE per-capita gonadic mass \(q(w,t)\) time step. derivation PDE given gonadic_mass_dynamics vignette; result tridiagonal linear system form \[a_{ij}\, q_{j-1} + b_{ij}\, q_j = s_{ij}\] coefficients : system solved calling mizer’s internal C++ routine: ::: call intentional: inner_project_loop forward-sweep solver mizer uses fish number density, public wrapper . creates hard dependency mizer’s internal layout, future mizer refactor renames moves function break gonadDynamics. dependency accepted () physics two PDEs identical, reusing solver correct, (b) alternative duplicate several hundred lines compiled C++.
code: # a_{ij} = -g_i(w_j) * dt / dw_j       (sub-diagonal: growth advection) a <- sweep(-rates$e_growth * dt, 2, params@dw, "/")  # b_{ij} = 1 + g_i(w_j) * dt / dw_j + r_i * dt   (diagonal: advection + release) b <- 1 + sweep(rates$e_growth * dt, 2, params@dw, "/") + r * dt  # s_{ij} = q_{ij} + e_repro_{ij} * dt   (right-hand side: old state + investment) s <- n_other$gonads + rates$e_repro * dt mizer:::inner_project_loop(no_sp = no_sp, no_w = no_w,                             n = n_other$gonads,                             A = a, B = b, S = s,                             w_min_idx = params@w_min_idx)

title: Code structure
text: four release functions share signature: return numeric vector length equal number species, giving mass-specific gonad release rate \(r_i(t)\) species \(\) time \(t\).

title: Code structure
text: release functions produce rate periodic period exactly one year. achieve slightly different ways: trunc() floor() agree positive arguments (truncate towards zero), last two equivalent practice. inconsistency historical artefact rather deliberate design choice.

what: The von Mises rate at the seasonal peak and trough
title: Code structure
text: von Mises release rate \[r(t) = r_0 \frac{e^{\kappa \cos(2\pi(t-\mu))}}{2\pi I_0(\kappa)}\] cosine reaches maximum \(+1\) \(t = \mu\) minimum \(-1\) \(t = \mu + 0.5\), : \[r(\mu) = \frac{r_0\, e^{\kappa}}{2\pi I_0(\kappa)}, \qquad   r(\mu + 0.5) = \frac{r_0\, e^{-\kappa}}{2\pi I_0(\kappa)}\] ratio peak trough rate \(e^{2\kappa}\), concentration \(\kappa = 2\) gives ratio \(54\).

title: Code structure
text: function time-varying version mizer’s built-BevertonHoltRDD. applies formula \[R_{dd}(t) = \frac{R_{di}(t)\, R_{\max}(t)}{R_{di}(t) + R_{\max}(t)}\] \(R_{\max}(t)\) looked 2-D array stored other_params(params)$r_max. array must time dimension dimnames: time lookup converts fractional year character key: Two practical consequences follow design: r_max array must pre-populated every time step simulation evaluated (.e., every multiple dt within [0, 1)). Missing entries cause explicit stop(). Floating-point rounding .character(round(x, 5)) can produce surprising keys. example, .character(round(0.1, 5)) "0.1" platforms, small floating-point errors simulation clock push t - floor(t) something like 0.09999999999 rounds "0.1". typically safe, worth aware dt small (e.g., dt = 0.001).
code: # Required structure of other_params(params)$r_max: times <- seq(0, 0.9, by = 0.1) r_max <- matrix(     values,     nrow = length(times), ncol = no_sp,     dimnames = list(time = as.character(round(times, 5)),                     species = sp_names) ) other_params(params)$r_max <- r_max t_name <- as.character(round(t - floor(t), 5)) r_max  <- other_params(params)$r_max[t_name, ]

title: Code structure
text: function calculates von Mises-distributed reproduction rate independent actual egg production. intended use placeholder model calibration (proper \(R_{\max}\) estimated). signature : Mizer calls RDD functions : rdi seasonalVonMisesRDD’s formal argument list, silently absorbed ... ignored. works, means : function used calibrated simulation: ignores egg production entirely can produce densities inconsistent gonadic dynamics. documentation explicitly warns “replaced another reproduction rate function simulating dynamics.”
code: seasonalVonMisesRDD <- function(params, t, ...) do.call(params@rates_funcs$RDD,         list(rdi = rdi, params = params, t = t, ...))

title: Code structure
text: S3 method replaces mizer’s standard energy-based RDI calculation entirely. call NextMethod(). Instead computes egg production directly gonadic release: r * gonads * n * dw integrates give total gonadic mass released per unit time per species. Dividing egg weight multiplying erepro / 2 (female fraction × reproductive efficiency) converts mass egg numbers. e_growth, mort, e_repro arguments present generic compatibility used.
code: projectRDI.mizerSeasonal <- function(params, n, n_pp, n_other, t = 0,                                      e_growth, mort, e_repro, ...) {     release_func <- get0(other_params(params)$release_func)     r <- release_func(t, params)            # mass-specific release rate (species vector)     total <- drop((sweep(n_other$gonads, 1, r, "*") * n) %*% params@dw)     0.5 * (total * params@species_params$erepro) /         params@w[params@w_min_idx] }

title: Code structure
text: S3 method additive: calls NextMethod() get standard encounter rate appends extra encounter arising gonadic mass carried prey fish. approach mathematically exact encounter integral linear prey biomass: \[E(w_\text{somatic} + q) = E(w_\text{somatic}) + E(q)\] two contributions can computed separately summed. gonad contribution added include_gonads = TRUE (set setSeasonalReproduction). FALSE, method returns NextMethod() unchanged. Note gonad contribution zero predator–prey interaction matrix entry predator–prey species pair zero. single-species model built newSingleSpeciesParams() interaction matrix interaction[1,1] = 0 default (self-predation), gonad contribution exactly zero projectEncounter.mizerSeasonal returns result mizerEncounter. observe difference need least one nonzero fish--fish interaction. method contains two code paths — one using FFT convolution (fast default) one using direct matrix multiplication (used user supplied custom pred_kernel). FFT path, prey_gonad swept dw_full (w_full * dw_full) gonads already represents absolute mass rather mass density \(w \cdot N(w)\) used somatic biomass.

title: Code structure
text: function modulates resource carrying capacity von Mises seasonal signal. reads parameters named sub-list rp resource_params slot: differs release functions receive parameters (via species_params columns). choice resource_params$rp resource species dimension, species_params appropriate.

title: Code structure
text: getTimeseries(sim, func) loops saved time steps MizerSim object calls func step, reconstructing per-time-step state vectors mizer store directly. reconstruction follows pattern mizer uses internally: func argument defaults mizer::getRDI, getTimeseries(sim) returns RDI time series. Pass mizer::getRDD get RDD time series, plotRDD .
code: for (i in time_indices) {     n <- sim@n[i, , ]     dim(n) <- dim(params@initial_n)          # restore matrix shape     n_other <- sim@n_other[i, ]              # named list of other components     names(n_other) <- dimnames(sim@n_other)[[2]]     value[i, ] <- func(params,                        n = n,                        n_pp = sim@n_pp[i, ],                        n_other = n_other,                        t = times[i]) }

title: Code structure
text: plotRDI plotRDD delegate data extraction getTimeseries rely mizer’s internal plotDataFrame helper actual rendering. data frame passed plotDataFrame must columns specific order (Year, Value, Species), functions explicit column reorder: plotGonadsVsTime simpler base-graphics function plots per-capita gonadic mass species’ maximum body size time. time_range supplied two-element vector interpreted (t_min, t_max) passed mizer’s get_time_elements(), returns logical index vector saved times — count. number selected time steps therefore sum(time_elements), length(time_elements), code must use length(qf) (number selected gonad matrices) row dimension output matrix:
code: plot_dat <- plot_dat[, c(1, 3, 2)]  # reorder to (Year, Value, Species) qf     <- sim@n_other[time_elements, "gonads"]   # list, length = sum(time_elements) qs_mat <- matrix(..., nrow = length(qf), ...)    # NOT length(time_elements)

title: Code structure
text: tests live tests/testthat/ organised follows: test-extension.R uses local_params() helper (built NS_species_params) rather shared seasonal_params fixture. keeps extension-mechanism tests self-contained avoids depending single-species model shared fixture uses. fixture seasonal_params uses seasonalVonMisesRDD RDD function (rather default seasonalBevertonHoltRDD) avoid construct r_max time-series array seasonalBevertonHoltRDD requires. Tests seasonalBevertonHoltRDD construct minimal r_max inline. fixture seasonal_sim produced project(seasonal_params, t_max = 2, dt = 0.1, t_save = 0.5), giving five saved time points (0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2). Keeping t_max small t_save coarse makes helper fast load without sacrificing coverage plot functions need MizerSim object.

what: Deriving the PDE for gonadic mass
title: Gonadic mass dynamics
text: Standard mizer describes number density \(N(w,t)\) PDE \[ \frac{\partial N}{\partial t} = -\frac{\partial}{\partial w}(g\,N)-\mu\,N, \tag{1}\] \(g(w,t)\) growth rate individual size \(w\) \(\mu(w,t)\) death rate. equation simply states rate change number individuals infinitesimal size range around \(w\) given difference rate individuals grow size range rate grow (transport term), minus rate die (loss term). Instead number density \(N\) used biomass density \(B = w\,N\) describe population. evolution equation can easily derived Equation 1 \[\begin{split} \frac{\partial B}{\partial t} &= w\frac{\partial N}{\partial t} = -w\frac{\partial}{\partial w}\left(g\,\frac{B}{w}\right)-\mu\,B,\\ &=-\frac{\partial}{\partial w}\left(g\,B\right)+g\,\frac{B}{w}-\mu\,B. \end{split}\] intuitive: addition transport term loss term now also source term \(g B/w = gN\) representing coming growth taking place within infinitesimal size class. now denote \(Q(w,t)\) total gonadic biomass density \(G(w,t)\) gonadic growth rate individual size \(w\) (reproducing), satisfy analogous equation \[ \frac{\partial Q}{\partial t} =-\frac{\partial}{\partial w}\left(g\,Q\right)+G\,N-\mu\,Q-r\,Q, \] additional loss term representing release gonadic mass reproduction. release rate \(r(w,t)\) encode seasonality reproduction. mizer, total energy \(E_r\) available growth reproduction split \(g=E_r(1-\psi)\) \(G=E_r\psi\). Thus \(G=g\,\psi/(1-\psi)\). Instead considering total gonadic biomass density \(Q(w,t)\), consider gonadic mass \(q(w,t)\) individual fish size \(w\), given \[ q= \frac{Q}{N}. \] Let’s derive evolution PDE: \[\begin{split} \frac{\partial q}{\partial t} &= \frac{1}{N}\frac{\partial Q}{\partial t}- \frac{Q}{N^2}\frac{\partial N}{\partial t}\\ &=\frac{1}{N}\left(-\frac{\partial}{\partial w}\left(g\,Q\right)+G\,N-\mu\,Q-r\,Q\right) -\frac{Q}{N^2}\left(-\frac{\partial}{\partial w}(g\,N)-\mu\,N\right). \end{split} \tag{2}\] simplify observe two mortality terms cancel \[\begin{split} \frac{Q}{N^2}\frac{\partial}{\partial w}(g\,N) &=\frac{q}{N}\frac{\partial}{\partial w}\left(g\,\frac{Q}{q}\right)\\ &=\frac{1}{N}\frac{\partial}{\partial w}\left(g\,Q\right) -g\,\frac{\partial q}{\partial w}. \end{split}\] Substituting Equation 2 cancelling terms gives \[ \frac{\partial q}{\partial t}=G-g\,\frac{\partial q}{\partial w}-r\, q. \tag{3}\] point feel bit stupid realise \[ \frac{dq(w,t)}{dt}=\frac{\partial q(w,t)}{\partial t}+ \frac{\partial q(w,t)}{\partial w}\frac{dw}{dt} \] \(dw/dt = g\), Equation 3 reduces \[ \frac{dq}{dt}=G-r\,q, \tag{4}\] written immediately, given definition \(G\). equation good starting point deriving PDE Equation 3. equations species specific. particular one gonadic mass function \(q_i(w,t)\) species \(\) need specify one rate function \(r_i(w,t)\) species \(\). need specify mass-specific release rate \(r(w,t)\). determines total reproduction rate \[\begin{equation} R_p(t)=\frac{\epsilon}{2w_0}\int N(w,t)q(w,t)r(w,t)dw. \end{equation}\] difficult know choose mass-specific rate \(r(w,t)\) produce observed egg production rate \(R_p(t)\). ’ll probably trial error initially. rate \(r(w,t)\) become large least year gonadic mass goes back close zero. One possibility make \(r(w,t)\) independent \(w\).

title: Gonadic mass dynamics
text: solve PDE Equation 3 use upwind-difference scheme used mizer solve PDE Equation 1. Let us therefore recall details scheme. discretise Equation 1 follows: \[     \frac{N_w^{t+1}-N_w^t}{\Delta t}=-\frac{g_w^tN_w^{t+1}-g_{w-1}^tN_{w-1}^{t+1}}{\Delta w}-\mu_w^t\,N_w^{t+1}. \tag{5}\] use \(w\) \(t\) denote integer indices discretised weight discretised time respectively. Multiplying \(\Delta t\) collecting terms gives \[     N_w^{t+1}\left(1+g_w^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w}+\mu_w^t\,\Delta t\right)=     N_{w-1}^{t+1}\left(g_{w-1}^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w}\right)     +N_w^t. \tag{6}\] Solving \(N_w^{t+1}\) gives \[     N_w^{t+1}=\frac{S_w^t-A_w^tN_{w-1}^{t+1}}{B_w^t}, \tag{7}\] \[ \begin{split}    A_w^t&=-g_{w-1}^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w},\\    B_w^t&=1+g_w^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w}+\mu^t_w\,\Delta t,\\    S_w^t&=N_w^t. \end{split} \tag{8}\] Given initial value boundary condition encoding influx new-born individuals smallest size class, recurrence relation Equation 7 allows mizer calculate \(N_w^{t+1}\) size classes later times. Now use scheme gonadic dynamics. discretise Equation 3 follows: \[     \frac{q_w^{t+1}-q_w^t}{\Delta t}=-g_w^t\frac{q_w^{t+1}-q_{w-1}^{t+1}}{\Delta w}-r_w^t\,q_w^{t+1}+G_w^t. \] Note close equation \(q\) equation Equation 5 \(N\). Multiplying \(\Delta t\) collecting terms gives \[     q_w^{t+1}\left(1+g_w^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w}+r_w^t\,\Delta t\right)=     q_{w-1}^{t+1}\left(g_w^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w}\right)     +q_w^t+G_w^t\,\Delta t. \] Solving \(q_w^{t+1}\) gives \[     q_w^{t+1}=\frac{s_w^t-a_w^tq_{w-1}^{t+1}}{b_w^t}, \tag{9}\] \[ \begin{split}    a_w^t&=-g_w^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w},\\    b_w^t&=1+g_w^t\frac{\Delta t}{\Delta w}+r_w^t\,\Delta t,\\    s_w^t&=q_w^t+G_w^t\,\Delta t. \end{split} \tag{10}\] Together initial value boundary condition \(q_1^t=0\) times smallest individuals yet gonadic mass, recurrence relation allows us calculate \(q_w^{t+1}\) size classes later times. Note small difference discretised equation Equation 9 \(q\) equation Equation 7 \(N\). difference lies replacing \(A_w^t, B_w^t\) \(S_w^t\) given Equation 8 \(a_w^t, b_w^t\) \(s_w^t\) given Equation 10.

title: Gonadic mass dynamics
text: modelling cohorts mizer, diffusion terms size-spectrum dynamics become important, lead broadening cohorts. yet know required step sizes size time faithfully reproduce dynamics. large size steps lead much numerical diffusion. large time steps may lead numerical instability.

title: Parameters used by Datta & Blanchard (2016) — datta_params
text: paper "effects seasonal processes size spectrum dynamics" Samik Datta Julia Blanchard, published Can. J. Fish. Aquat. Sci. 73, 598–610 (2016), first paper investigate seasonal processes context size spectrum dynamics. authors used early version mizer code modified. code available https://figshare.com/s/e75f29d4cc9b94ae393b. MizerParams object based parameters used paper. However choice size bins numerical implementation mizer equations different one used paper. paper used wider size-bins resource spectrum fish spectrum. modern mizer code longer supports . MizerParams object uses size bins resource fish spectra. Like model Datta Blanchard, use 100 size bins fish spectra use 180 size bins full spectra instead 130. initial state MizerParams object set steady state model. script created MizerParams object available https://github.com/gustavdelius/mizerSeasonal/blob/main/data-raw/datta_params.R.

what: Beta hazard mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalBetaHazardRelease
title: Beta hazard mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalBetaHazardRelease
text: Uses formula $$r(w, t) = r(t) = \frac{f(t-\lfloor t \rfloor)}{1 - F(t-\lfloor t \rfloor)}$$ \(f(t)\) \(F(t)\) probability density function cumulative distribution function beta distribution (see see ?dbeta) parameters shape1 = beta_a shape2 = beta_b. mizer measures time years, \(t-\lfloor t \rfloor\) gives time within year \(r(t)\) periodic function period one year.

title: Beta hazard mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalBetaHazardRelease
Beta hazard mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalBetaHazardRelease
text: t time calculate reproduction rate params MizerParams object

title: Beta hazard mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalBetaHazardRelease
text: vector species-specific release rates time t

what: Beta distributed gonad release rate — seasonalBetaRelease
title: Beta distributed gonad release rate — seasonalBetaRelease
text: Uses formula $$r(w, t) = r(t) = f(t-\lfloor t \rfloor)$$ \(f(t)\) probability density function beta distribution (see see ?dbeta) parameters shape1 = beta_a shape2 = beta_b. mizer measures time years, \(t-\lfloor t \rfloor\) gives time within year \(r(t)\) periodic function period one year.

what: Beverton Holt function to calculate density-dependent reproduction rate — seasonalBevertonHoltRDD
title: Beverton Holt function to calculate density-dependent reproduction rate — seasonalBevertonHoltRDD
text: Takes density-independent rates \(R_{di}(t)\) egg production returns reduced, density-dependent reproduction rates \(R_{dd}(t)\) given $$R_{dd}(t) = R_{di}(t) \frac{R_{max}(t)}{R_{di}(t) + R_{max}(t)}$$ \(R_{max}(t)\) maximum possible reproduction rates must specified array (time x species) saved other_params$R_max slot MizerParams object. simply time-dependent version mizer::BevertonHoltRDD(). (quantities equation species-specific dropped species index simplicity.)

title: Beverton Holt function to calculate density-dependent reproduction rate — seasonalBevertonHoltRDD

title: Get encounter rate that includes gonadic mass of prey — seasonalEncounter
text: mizerEncounter() function core mizer except prey mass sum somatic mass \(w_p\) gonadic mass \(q(w_p)\). function automatically registered model's encounter rate function setSeasonalReproduction() called include_gonads = TRUE.

what: Gaussian mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalGaussianRelease
title: Gaussian mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalGaussianRelease
text: Uses formula $$r(w, t) = r(t) = r_0 \exp{\left(-\dfrac{(t-\lfloor{t}\rfloor-t_0)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right)}$$ parameters \(r_0, \sigma\) \(t_0\) given new columns sr_r0, sr_sigma sr_t0 species parameter data frame. mizer measures time years, \(t-\lfloor t \rfloor\) gives time within year \(r(t)\) periodic function period one year.

title: Gaussian mass-specific gonad release rate — seasonalGaussianRelease

text: vector species-specific release rates time t

what: Get density-independent rate of seasonal reproduction — seasonalRDI
title: Get density-independent rate of seasonal reproduction — seasonalRDI
text: function calculates density-independent rate offspring production species. rate offspring production given $$R_{di}(t) =\frac{\epsilon}{2w_0}\int N(w,t)q(w,t)r(w,t)dw$$ \(q(w,t)\) gonadic mass individual, \(r(w,t)\) mass-specific gonad release rate, \(N(w,t)\) abundance density, \(\epsilon\) reproductive efficiency \(w_0\) weight offspring.

title: Get density-independent rate of seasonal reproduction — seasonalRDI

title: Get density-independent rate of seasonal reproduction — seasonalRDI
text: numeric vector rate egg production species.

what: von-Mises distributed reproduction rate independent of abundance — seasonalVonMisesRDD
title: von-Mises distributed reproduction rate independent of abundance — seasonalVonMisesRDD
text: function calculates mass-specific reproduction rate species given time. reproduction rate given $$r(t) = r_0 \frac{\exp(\kappa \cos(2\pi(t - \mu)))}{2\pi I_0(\kappa)}$$ \(r_0\) amplitude reproduction rate, \(\kappa\) concentration parameter von-Mises distribution, \(\mu\) mean von-Mises distribution. parameters must supplied slots rdd_vonMises_r0, rdd_vonMises_kappa rdd_vonMises_mu species_params data frame params object.

title: von-Mises distributed reproduction rate independent of abundance — seasonalVonMisesRDD

title: Seasonal semichemostat resource dynamics — seasonal_resource_semichemostat
text: implements standard semichemostat dynamics resource (see mizer::resource_semichemostat()) time-dependent carrying capacity. carrying capacity given $$c_R(w,t) = (1 + \text{maxR} \cdot \text{vonMises}(t, \mu, \kappa)) c_R(w)$$ \(c_R(w)\) standard carrying capacity, \(\mu\) mean von-Mises distribution, \(\kappa\) concentration parameter von-Mises distribution. parameters must supplied rp$maxR, rp$mu rp$kappa slots resource_params slot params object.

title: Set seasonal reproduction — setSeasonalReproduction
text: returns new model reproduction rate varies throughout year. See details .

title: Set seasonal reproduction — setSeasonalReproduction
code: setSeasonalReproduction(   params,   release_func = "seasonalVonMisesRelease",   RDD = "seasonalBevertonHoltRDD",   include_gonads = TRUE )

title: Set seasonal reproduction — setSeasonalReproduction
text: params MizerParams object release_func Name function giving time-dependent mass-specific release rate. function take time MizerParams object arguments return vector reproduction rates species. RDD Name function calculating density-dependent reproduction rate RDD. include_gonads Boolean. TRUE (default) gonadic mass included prey encounter rate.

title: Set seasonal reproduction — setSeasonalReproduction
text: MizerParams object seasonal reproduction

title: Set seasonal reproduction — setSeasonalReproduction
text: function change rate fish invest energy reproduction. however changes happens investment. Rather released immediately produce offspring, used accumulate gonadic mass. specify function gives time-dependent mass-specific rate \(r(t)\) gonadic mass released reproduction. package provides several candidate functions calculating release rates: seasonalVonMisesRelease(), seasonalBetaHazardRelease(), seasonalBetaRelease() seasonalGaussianRelease(), can use templates writing release functions. also specify non-linear function calculates density-dependent reproduction rate \(R_{dd}\) density-independent rate \(R_{di}\) egg production.
